examples.title: Algorithm: Convolution Operation
examples.description: Performs the convolution operation between an image and a kernel.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Convolution
INPUT: Image I, Kernel K
OUTPUT: Convolved Image

BEGIN
  FOR each pixel (x, y) in I
    Initialize sum = 0
    FOR each kernel element (i, j) in K
      IF (x-i, y-j) is within I
        sum += I(x-i, y-j) * K(i, j)
    END FOR
    Convolved Image(x, y) = sum
  END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the convolution operation between an image and a kernel. It iterates over each pixel in the image, computes the weighted sum of neighboring pixels using the kernel, and stores the result in the convolved image.

examples.title: Python: Convolution Operation
examples.description: Performs the convolution operation between an image and a kernel using Python.
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the convolution operation between an image and a kernel. It uses NumPy arrays to represent the image and kernel and iterates over each pixel in the image to compute the weighted sum of neighboring pixels using the kernel.

examples.title: C: Convolution Operation
examples.description: Performs the convolution operation between an image and a kernel using C.
examples.explanation: This C code implements the convolution operation between an image and a kernel. It uses dynamic memory allocation to create the convolved image and iterates over each pixel in the image to compute the weighted sum of neighboring pixels using the kernel.
